cause: the design of record claimed the new tab/workspace-ratio scenario closes a gap around the `!window &&` guard on tmux's size flag, and that framing was wrong. The guard is structurally REDUNDANT: size is only spread into the split-window branches and the new-window branch never references it, so deleting the guard changes no emitted argv. Caught by running the mutation the plan's own verification step demanded rather than by reading: removing the guard left all 52 tests green, which is exactly the signature of an inert scenario and would have been reported as one at the impl gate.
fix: re-ran the mutation against the RIGHT wrong subject — an adapter author who wires ...size into the window branch — and confirmed the scenario registers a miss: with size spread into new-window and the guard dropped, both rows of the scenario fail. The scenario is kept because it is loseable and pins the contract the README states (a tab or workspace is never sized); what changed is the account of WHY. The design doc's D7 now records that tmux is defended twice over, that it takes breaking both defenses to lose the row, and that the scenario is tmux-only because herdr's size is lexically scoped inside its pane-split branch and cannot fail it at all. No scenario, test assertion, or production code changed as a result — only the claim about what is under test.

cause: cold impl-judge round 1 returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false on a real would-have-shipped gap: the src/session.ts doc-comment correction was NEVER COMMITTED. The spec commit's own message claims 'Also corrects session.ts... Comment only, no behavior change', but the staging line added only the spec directory and the plan files, so session.ts stayed as uncommitted working-tree drift through all three commits. At HEAD the shipped interface doc still said env is 'only meaningful for a pane:* placement' — directly contradicting the scenario this CR had just FROZEN, which says env reaches every tier. The commit message asserted a fix that was not in the tree. The judge caught it by running git show --name-only over each commit rather than reading the message, which is the only way this was visible.
fix: session.ts committed in its own unit, so the contract and the interface doc that documents it agree at HEAD. The lesson is the staging, not the edit: a `git add <dir>` line silently dropped a file the message spoke for, and nothing in the suite could catch it because the change is a comment. Re-verified by re-running git show --name-only over the new commit rather than trusting the message a second time.

cause: cold impl-judge round 1 also found the herdr test bound to 'env with no launch opens a blank shell carrying the env' under-verifies its own scenario. The scenario says nothing is typed, sent, OR run; the herdr test asserted only the absence of `pane run`, never `pane send-text` or `pane send-keys`. herdr spreads typing across three verbs where tmux funnels everything through send-keys, so the tmux twin's single check is complete and the herdr one is not. The judge proved the blind spot by mutation rather than by reading: injecting a stray bare-Enter submit whenever env is set with no launch left all 73 herdr tests green. This is a rename-induced over-claim — the retitled test now promises more than its body checks.
fix: the assertion now rules out all three of herdr's input verbs (run/send-text/send-keys) rather than run alone, matching the tmux twin's strength. Re-ran the judge's own mutation to confirm the fix is not theatre: the stray submit now fails the scenario's test where it previously passed 73/73. Test-only change; no edit to session.herdr.ts and no edit to the frozen contract.

cause: cold impl-judge round 1 checked the sibling of a defect this mission had already self-caught and found the design doc's account still incomplete: the !window && guard on tmux's `from` (session.tmux.ts:25) is redundant for exactly the same reason as the one on `size` — the new-window branch never spreads ...from either — so removing it alone leaves the tmux suite green. The self-caught correction had documented the `size` case and stopped there, never asking whether the neighbouring guard had the same shape.
fix: design doc D7 now records both guards as the same dead defense-in-depth, and that each scenario is loseable only by the compound wrong subject (guard removed AND the value wired into the window branch). The guards are deliberately NOT removed: they cost nothing, state the intent locally, and deleting them would be a production change outside this spec-only CR's scope. Documentation-only.
